name: Linked List Cycle II
description: Given the head of a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null. Use Floyd's algorithm: after slow and fast meet, reset one pointer to head and advance both by one until they meet at the cycle start.

examples.output: Node with value 2 (cycle starts at index 1)

constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

performance.stress_intent: exercise long cycle-entry cases that punish restart-heavy searches instead of Floyd's algorithm or linear visited-set tracking
